"SEX1," entry-level condenser microphone, designed specifically outperform competitive models sub-$200 street price.
capsule described "hand-built."
large-diaphragm, center-terminated design with outside diameter 32mm.
Based photos we've seen, capsule backplates, characteristic drilling pattern.
rear diaphragm clear (not metallized).
circuit transformerless, built with combination surface-mount through-hole parts.
headbasket grille dual layer metal mesh (previously reported here, incorrectly, single layer) fine mesh screen lines inside grille.
face grille sloped reduce standing-wave coloration.
Switches front body provide -10dB high-pass filter.
filter appears operate higher cutoff frequency (approximately 500Hz), with milder slope (4dB/octave) than typical.
mic's frequency response nominally flat from 30Hz-3kHz, smoothly rising presence peak (+3dB) around 12kHz.
25.1mV/Pa, mic's sensitivity higher than many competitive models.
self-noise (16dBA) relatively low, making suitable recording quiet sources.
body finished non-glare, slightly rubberized paint, which likely reduces body resonance.
ships with clip only.
attaches tight plastic collar that snaps around stem housing jack.
flight case shockmount available separately.

Americans stripes oppose militarization U.S. police forces.
December 2013 Reason-Rupe poll found that Americans thought that police militarization gone far.
research poll shows that plurality people think that police have gone Ferguson, Missouri.
does Congress continue approve militarization?
same reason that Congress members vote spying Americans easy Wall Street criminals: money.
group's report looked June congressional vote legislation, offered U.S. Rep. Alan Grayson, D-Fla., that would have blocked Pentagon from spending resources transferring military hardware local police agencies.
bill defeated 62-355.
According data compiled Maplight, lawmakers "voting continue funding 1033 Program have received, average, percent more money from defense industry than representatives voting defund it."
all, average lawmaker voting against bill received more than $50,000 campaign donations from defense industry last years.
report also found that lawmakers received more than $100,000 from defense contractors last years, only four voted Grayson's legislation.

CERN's Large Hadron Collider (LHC) switched this morning ahead planned two-year-long upgrade, bringing conclusion very successful second most powerful particle accelerator world.
itself, CERN's accelerator complex experiments will undergo major renovations improvements preparation next starting 2021, High-Luminosity (HL-LHC) project which start after 2025 will allow produce even more data.
performed beyond expectations during second run, which started 2015, producing five times more data than first run.
This time round, produced approximately million-billion proton-proton collisions, with more than petabytes data permanently archived equivalent 1,000 years 24/7 video streaming.
Analysing this data helped expand knowledge fundamental physics Universe.
particular, been helping understand Higgs boson, which confirmed CERN 2012, behaves accordance with Standard Model theory that describes best understanding elementary particles.
